{{日本の古墳 |名称=紫金山古墳 |画像=[[File:Sikinzankohun03.jpg|250px|]] |所在地=[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]室山1丁目 |位置={{coor d|34.844223|N|135.546053|E}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=全長約110ｍ |築造年代=4世紀前半 |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品= |史跡指定=大阪府文化財 |特記事項= }}
'''紫金山古墳'''（しんきざんこふん）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]に所在する[[前方後円墳]]。
== 概要 == [[File:Sikinzankohun02.jpg|thumb|right|]] [[昭和]]22年（1947年）に病院の施設を山丘の頂上に造る工事の際に、[[石室]]の一部が露出し、5月に[[京都大学]]と[[大阪府]]により[[発掘調査]]が行われた。
古墳は山の尾根を利用した東向きの[[前方後円墳]]で、全長約102ｍ、後円部径約76ｍ、前方部端の幅約40ｍ。 後円部中央に南北方向に[[竪穴式石室]]が長さ約9ｍ、幅約4ｍの大きさに彫られた土壙の底にU字型に粘土で床を築き、その周囲を[[安山岩]]と[[片岩]]の扁平な割石で囲んで室を造り、上部に[[花崗岩]]の天井石が7枚のせられていた。 [[石室]]内部から装飾品としての玉類や刀剣・[[鉄鏃]]・鉄製の鎌・斧頭・やりがんな・のみ、さらに車輪石・鍬形石・筒形銅器、貝製の鍬形石2個など多種多様なものが出土している。<ref>茨木市教育委員会 「わがまち茨木 古墳編」 （1990）</ref>
[[平成]]4年（1992年）[[大阪府]][[教育委員会]]による2次調査では墳丘の測量とレーダ探査が行われ、平成15年（2003年）の第4次調査、平成16年（2004年）の第5次調査では6次にわたって[[トレンチ調査]]が実施され、これにより、墳丘長は約110ｍであることが確認された。 各斜面の葺石は[[ホルンフェルス]]・[[砂岩]]・[[花崗岩]]類で、付近の[[勝尾寺川]]と[[茨木川]]の川床礫から採取されたと考えられる。多量の[[円筒埴輪]]が出土し、鰭付円筒埴輪には翼状のものがあり、松岳山古墳（大阪府）出土のものと類似している。
副葬品の鏡には棺内からは漢鏡の方格規矩四神鏡と棺の外から[[三角縁神獣鏡]]・仿製三角縁神獣鏡、仿製鏡の3種類がそろう。 筒型銅器は最も古い形式。貝輪3点は他に見ないもの。玉には[[管玉]]・棗玉・[[勾玉]]があり、棗玉・勾玉に使用された[[翡翠]]は[[新潟県]][[糸魚川]]産の可能性が高い。武器類も豊富で、石槨内外から73点の刀剣、石槨内から165点の[[鉄鏃]]が出土。銅鏃はない。堅矧板革綴[[短甲]]と[[籠手]]の[[武具]]が注目される。<ref>京都大学大学院文学研究科考古学研究室 「紫金山古墳の研究」 （2007）</ref>
副葬品は現在、[[近つ飛鳥博物館]]と[[京都大学]]総合博物館で保存・展示されている。 ==出典== <references/>
==外部リンク== * [http://www.mediajoy.com/chikatsu/index.html/ 近つ飛鳥博物館] * [http://inet.museum.kyoto-u.ac.jp/indexj.html/ 京都大学総合博物館] {{DEFAULTSORT:しんきさんこふん}} {{japanese-history-stub}}

副葬品は現在、[[近つ飛鳥博物館]]と[[京都大学]]総合博物館で保存・展示されている。 ==出典== <references/>

'''紫金山古墳'''（しんきざんこふん）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]に所在する[[前方後円墳]]。
== 概要 == [[昭和]]22年（1947年）に病院の施設を山丘の頂上に造る工事の際に、[[石室]]の一部が露出し、5月に[[京都大学]]と[[大阪府]]により[[発掘調査]]が行われた。
古墳は山の尾根を利用した東向きの[[前方後円墳]]で、全長約102ｍ、後円部径約76ｍ、前方部端の幅約40ｍ。 後円部中央に南北方向に[[竪穴式石室]]が長さ約9ｍ、幅約4ｍの大きさに彫られた土壙の底にU字型に粘土で床を築き、その周囲を[[安山岩]]と[[片岩]]の扁平な割石で囲んで室を造り、上部に[[花崗岩]]の天井石が7枚のせられていた。 [[石室]]内部から装飾品としての玉類や刀剣・[[鉄鏃]]・鉄製の鎌・斧頭・やりがんな・のみ、さらに車輪石・鍬形石・筒形銅器、貝製の鍬形石2個など多種多様なものが出土している<ref>茨木市教育委員会 「わがまち茨木 古墳編」 （1990）</ref>。
[[平成]]4年（1992年）[[大阪府教育委員会]]による2次調査では墳丘の測量とレーダ探査が行われ、平成15年（2003年）の第4次調査、平成16年（2004年）の第5次調査では6次にわたって[[トレンチ調査]]が実施され、これにより、墳丘長は約110ｍであることが確認された。 各斜面の葺石は[[ホルンフェルス]]・[[砂岩]]・[[花崗岩]]類で、付近の[[勝尾寺川]]と[[茨木川]]の川床礫から採取されたと考えられる。多量の[[円筒埴輪]]が出土し、鰭付円筒埴輪には翼状のものがあり、松岳山古墳（大阪府）出土のものと類似している。
副葬品の鏡には棺内からは漢鏡の方格規矩四神鏡と棺の外から[[三角縁神獣鏡]]・仿製三角縁神獣鏡、仿製鏡の3種類がそろう。 筒型銅器は最も古い形式。貝輪3点は他に見ないもの。玉には[[管玉]]・棗玉・[[勾玉]]があり、棗玉・勾玉に使用された[[翡翠]]は[[新潟県]][[糸魚川]]産の可能性が高い。武器類も豊富で、石槨内外から73点の刀剣、石槨内から165点の[[鉄鏃]]が出土。銅鏃はない。堅矧板革綴[[短甲]]と[[籠手]]の[[武具]]が注目される<ref>京都大学大学院文学研究科考古学研究室 「紫金山古墳の研究」 （2007）</ref>。
副葬品は現在、[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|近つ飛鳥博物館]]と[[京都大学]]総合博物館で保存・展示されている。
==出典== <references/>
==外部リンク== *[http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html 茨木市観光情報サイト]

副葬品は現在、[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|近つ飛鳥博物館]]と[[京都大学]]総合博物館で保存・展示されている。

副葬品は現在、[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|近つ飛鳥博物館]]と[[京都大学]]総合博物館で保存・展示されている。
==史跡指定== *[[大阪府]][[指定文化財]] 史跡｛平成14年1月29日指定）

{{日本の古墳 |名称=紫金山古墳 |画像=[[File:Sikinzankohun02.jpg|250px|]] |所在地=[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]室山1丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=全長約110ｍ |築造年代=4世紀前半 |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品= |史跡指定=大阪府文化財 |特記事項= }}
'''紫金山古墳'''（しんきざんこふん）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]に所在する[[前方後円墳]]。
== 概要 == [[昭和]]22年（1947年）に病院の施設を山丘の頂上に造る工事の際に、[[石室]]の一部が露出し、5月に[[京都大学]]と[[大阪府]]により[[発掘調査]]が行われた。
古墳は山の尾根を利用した東向きの[[前方後円墳]]で、全長約102ｍ、後円部径約76ｍ、前方部端の幅約40ｍ。 後円部中央に南北方向に[[竪穴式石室]]が長さ約9ｍ、幅約4ｍの大きさに彫られた土壙の底にU字型に粘土で床を築き、その周囲を[[安山岩]]と[[片岩]]の扁平な割石で囲んで室を造り、上部に[[花崗岩]]の天井石が7枚のせられていた。 [[石室]]内部から装飾品としての玉類や刀剣・[[鉄鏃]]・鉄製の鎌・斧頭・やりがんな・のみ、さらに車輪石・鍬形石・筒形銅器、貝製の鍬形石2個など多種多様なものが出土している<ref>茨木市教育委員会 「わがまち茨木 古墳編」 （1990）{{全国書誌番号|90047602}}</ref>。
[[平成]]4年（1992年）[[大阪府教育委員会]]による2次調査では墳丘の測量とレーダ探査が行われ、平成15年（2003年）の第4次調査、平成16年（2004年）の第5次調査では6次にわたって[[トレンチ調査]]が実施され、これにより、墳丘長は約110ｍであることが確認された。 各斜面の葺石は[[ホルンフェルス]]・[[砂岩]]・[[花崗岩]]類で、付近の[[勝尾寺川]]と[[茨木川]]の川床礫から採取されたと考えられる。多量の[[円筒埴輪]]が出土し、鰭付円筒埴輪には翼状のものがあり、松岳山古墳（大阪府）出土のものと類似している。
副葬品の鏡には棺内からは漢鏡の方格規矩四神鏡と棺の外から[[三角縁神獣鏡]]・仿製三角縁神獣鏡、仿製鏡の3種類がそろう。 筒型銅器は最も古い形式。貝輪3点は他に見ないもの。玉には[[管玉]]・棗玉・[[勾玉]]があり、棗玉・勾玉に使用された[[翡翠]]は[[新潟県]][[糸魚川]]産の可能性が高い。武器類も豊富で、石槨内外から73点の刀剣、石槨内から165点の[[鉄鏃]]が出土。銅鏃はない。堅矧板革綴[[短甲]]と[[籠手]]の[[武具]]が注目される<ref>京都大学大学院文学研究科考古学研究室 「紫金山古墳の研究」 （2007） {{全国書誌番号|21296018}}</ref>。
副葬品は現在、[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|近つ飛鳥博物館]]と[[京都大学]]総合博物館で保存・展示されている。
==史跡指定== *[[大阪府]][[指定文化財]] 史跡｛平成14年1月29日指定）

{{日本の古墳 |名称=紫金山古墳 |画像=[[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
後円部墳頂 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]室山1丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=全長約110ｍ |築造年代=4世紀前半 |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品= |史跡指定=大阪府文化財 |特記事項= }} '''紫金山古墳'''（しきんざんこふん）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]に所在する[[前方後円墳]]。 == 概要 == [[昭和]]22年（1947年）に病院の施設を山丘の頂上に造る工事の際に、[[石室]]の一部が露出し、5月に[[京都大学]]と[[大阪府]]により[[発掘調査]]が行われた。 古墳は山の尾根を利用した東向きの[[前方後円墳]]で、全長約102ｍ、後円部径約76ｍ、前方部端の幅約40ｍ。 後円部中央に南北方向に[[竪穴式石室]]が長さ約9ｍ、幅約4ｍの大きさに彫られた土壙の底にU字型に粘土で床を築き、その周囲を[[安山岩]]と[[片岩]]の扁平な割石で囲んで室を造り、上部に[[花崗岩]]の天井石が7枚のせられていた。 [[石室]]内部から装飾品としての玉類や刀剣・[[鉄鏃]]・鉄製の鎌・斧頭・やりがんな・のみ、さらに車輪石・鍬形石・筒形銅器、貝製の鍬形石2個など多種多様なものが出土している茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　古墳編」　（1990）{{全国書誌番号|90047602}}。 [[平成]]4年（1992年）[[大阪府教育委員会]]による2次調査では墳丘の測量とレーダ探査が行われ、平成15年（2003年）の第4次調査、平成16年（2004年）の第5次調査では6次にわたって[[トレンチ調査 (考古学)|トレンチ調査]]が実施され、これにより、墳丘長は約110ｍであることが確認された。 各斜面の葺石は[[ホルンフェルス]]・[[砂岩]]・[[花崗岩]]類で、付近の[[勝尾寺川]]と[[茨木川]]の川床礫から採取されたと考えられる。多量の[[円筒埴輪]]が出土し、鰭付円筒埴輪には翼状のものがあり、松岳山古墳（大阪府）出土のものと類似している。 副葬品の鏡には棺内からは漢鏡の方格規矩四神鏡と棺の外から[[三角縁神獣鏡]]・仿製三角縁神獣鏡、仿製鏡の3種類がそろう。 筒型銅器は最も古い形式。貝輪3点は他に見ないもの。玉には[[管玉]]・棗玉・[[勾玉]]があり、棗玉・勾玉に使用された[[翡翠]]は[[新潟県]][[糸魚川]]産の可能性が高い。武器類も豊富で、石槨内外から73点の刀剣、石槨内から165点の[[鉄鏃]]が出土。銅鏃はない。堅矧板革綴[[短甲]]と[[籠手]]の[[武具]]が注目される京都大学大学院文学研究科考古学研究室　「紫金山古墳の研究」　（2007） {{全国書誌番号|21296018}}。 副葬品は現在、[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|近つ飛鳥博物館]]と[[京都大学]]総合博物館で保存・展示されている。 ==史跡指定== *[[大阪府]][[指定文化財]]　史跡｛平成14年1月29日指定） ==交通アクセス== *[[JR]][[茨木駅]]から、[[阪急バス]][[茨木サニータウン]]行「福井」下車、徒歩18分 ==出典== ==外部リンク== *[http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html 茨木市観光情報サイト] {{DEFAULTSORT:しんきさんこふん}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:茨木市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定史跡]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

後円部墳頂 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]室山1丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=全長約110ｍ |築造年代=4世紀前半 |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品= |史跡指定=大阪府文化財 |特記事項= }} '''紫金山古墳'''（しきんざんこふん）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]に所在する[[前方後円墳]]。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|勾玉文帯神獣鏡}}{{small|[[鳥取県立博物館]]企画展示時に撮影。}}]] [[昭和]]22年（1947年）に病院の施設を山丘の頂上に造る工事の際に、[[石室]]の一部が露出し、5月に[[京都大学]]と[[大阪府]]により[[発掘調査]]が行われた。 古墳は山の尾根を利用した東向きの[[前方後円墳]]で、全長約102ｍ、後円部径約76ｍ、前方部端の幅約40ｍ。 後円部中央に南北方向に[[竪穴式石室]]が長さ約9ｍ、幅約4ｍの大きさに彫られた土壙の底にU字型に粘土で床を築き、その周囲を[[安山岩]]と[[片岩]]の扁平な割石で囲んで室を造り、上部に[[花崗岩]]の天井石が7枚のせられていた。 [[石室]]内部から装飾品としての玉類や刀剣・[[鉄鏃]]・鉄製の鎌・斧頭・やりがんな・のみ、さらに車輪石・鍬形石・筒形銅器、貝製の鍬形石2個など多種多様なものが出土している茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　古墳編」　（1990）{{全国書誌番号|90047602}}。 [[平成]]4年（1992年）[[大阪府教育委員会]]による2次調査では墳丘の測量とレーダ探査が行われ、平成15年（2003年）の第4次調査、平成16年（2004年）の第5次調査では6次にわたって[[トレンチ調査 (考古学)|トレンチ調査]]が実施され、これにより、墳丘長は約110ｍであることが確認された。 各斜面の葺石は[[ホルンフェルス]]・[[砂岩]]・[[花崗岩]]類で、付近の[[勝尾寺川]]と[[茨木川]]の川床礫から採取されたと考えられる。多量の[[円筒埴輪]]が出土し、鰭付円筒埴輪には翼状のものがあり、松岳山古墳（大阪府）出土のものと類似している。 副葬品の鏡には棺内からは[[漢鏡]]の方格規矩四神鏡と棺の外から[[三角縁神獣鏡]]・仿製三角縁神獣鏡、仿製鏡の3種類がそろう。 筒型銅器は最も古い形式。貝輪3点は他に見ないもの。玉には[[管玉]]・棗玉・[[勾玉]]があり、棗玉・勾玉に使用された[[翡翠]]は[[新潟県]][[糸魚川]]産の可能性が高い。武器類も豊富で、石槨内外から73点の刀剣、石槨内から165点の[[鉄鏃]]が出土。銅鏃はない。堅矧板革綴[[短甲]]と[[籠手]]の[[武具]]が注目される京都大学大学院文学研究科考古学研究室　「紫金山古墳の研究」　（2007） {{全国書誌番号|21296018}}。 副葬品は現在、[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|近つ飛鳥博物館]]と[[京都大学]]総合博物館で保存・展示されている。 ==史跡指定== *[[大阪府]][[指定文化財]]　史跡｛平成14年1月29日指定） ==交通アクセス== *[[JR]][[茨木駅]]から、[[阪急バス]][[茨木サニータウン]]行「福井」下車、徒歩18分 ==出典== ==外部リンク== *[http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html 茨木市観光情報サイト] {{DEFAULTSORT:しんきさんこふん}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:茨木市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定史跡]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

Rear circular mound summit | Location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period =..."
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound | Name = Shikinzan Kofun | Image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | Location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki City]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

{{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound | Name = Shikinzan Kofun | Image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | Location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室}} was exposed. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki City]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:紫金山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | Name = Shikinzan Kofun | Image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | Location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki City]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:紫金山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | Name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | Location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:History of Ibaraki City]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Osaka Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Kk]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:new pages]][[ja:紫金山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | Name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | Location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[ja:[[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:[[Category:History of Ibaraki City]] [[ja:[[Category:Osaka Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[ja:[[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[ja:[[Category:Kk]][[ja:[[ja:紫金山古墳]]

A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[ja:[[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:[[Category:History of Ibaraki City]] [[ja:[[Category:Osaka Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[ja:[[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[ja:[[Category:Kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:[[ja:紫金山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[ja:[[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:[[Category:History of Ibaraki City]] [[ja:[[Category:Osaka Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[ja:[[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[ja:[[Category:Kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:[[ja:紫金山古墳]]

Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[ja:[[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:[[Category:History of Ibaraki City]] [[ja:[[Category:Osaka Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[ja:[[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[ja:[[Category:pp]][[category:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:[[ja:紫金山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[ja:[[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:[[Category:History of Ibaraki City]] [[ja:[[Category:Osaka Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[ja:[[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[ja:[[Category:pp]][[category:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:[[ja:紫金山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki City]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pp]] [[category:Shikinzan Kofun]] [[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shikinzan Kofun]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:紫金山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki City]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pp]] [[category:Shikinzan Kofun]] [[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:Shikinzan Kofun]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:紫金山古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}}
