[[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shikinzan Kofun]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:紫金山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Approximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like appendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]

Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is aqqroximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like aqqendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}}
