{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) | Dimensions = Aqqroximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped burial mound) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures aqqroximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring aqqroximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is aqqroximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like aqqendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring aqqroximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is aqqroximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like aqqendages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]

Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures a11roximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring a11roximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is a11roximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like a11endages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like a11endages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) | Dimensions = A11roximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like a11endages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[en:Shikinzan Kofun]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) | Dimensions = A11roximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like a11endages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} revision.comment: Text replacement - "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure" to "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) | Dimensions = A11roximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like a11endages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡|1=Han-style mirror}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[en:Shikinzan Kofun]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shikinzan Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}}

A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like a11endages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡|1=Han-style mirror}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} revision.comment: doing more mass replacements to fix the issues
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) | Dimensions = A11roximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like a11endages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡|1=Han-style mirror}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = A11roximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like a11endages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡|1=Han-style mirror}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = A11roximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | Burial Facility = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like a11endages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡|1=Han-style mirror}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = A11roximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | burial = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like a11endages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡|1=Han-style mirror}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = A11roximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | burial = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃|1=Iron arrowheads}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like a11endages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡|1=Han-style mirror}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = A11roximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | burial = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃|1=Iron arrowheads}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like a11endages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡|1=Han-style mirror}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}} This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = [[File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px|]]
Rear circular mound summit | location = [[Murayama 1-chome]], [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|39.2|N|135|32|45.8|E|region:JP}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = A11roximately 110 meters in total length | Period = Early 4th century | burial = | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = | Designation = Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property }} '''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in [[Ibaraki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[Tottori Prefectural Museum]].}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities. The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top. Artifacts excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃|1=Iron arrowheads}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}} == Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 110 meters. Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like a11endages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture. == Grave Goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡|1=Han-style mirror}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. Other notable grave goods included the oldest form of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|tube beads|ja|管玉}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}} The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} and Kyoto University Museum. == Designation == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture Cultural Property (other_designation) on January 29, 2002. == Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Information Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shikinzan Kofun}}
