So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg | location = {{ill|Ibaraki|WD=Q502403}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.844222|N|135.546056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Approximately110 meters in total length | built = Early 4th century | burial = | Interred_individual = | burial goods = | designation1 = Osaka Prefecture designation1 }}
'''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in [[Ibaraki City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Tottori Prefectural Museum|en|Tottori Prefectural Museum|WD=Q4707652|lt=Tottori Prefectural Museum}}.}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|en|Kyoto University|WD=Q336264|lt=Kyoto University}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities.
The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top.
burial goods excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃|1=Iron arrowheads|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron arrowheads|qq=}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.<ref>Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}}</ref>
== Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters.
Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuoji River|1=Katsuōji River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400222}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki River|1=Ibaraki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617858}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like a11endages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture.
== burial goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡|1=Han-style mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese bronze mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Han-style mirror|qq=}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.
Other notable grave goods included the oldest type of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|Kudatama|lt=Kudatama|lt=Tube beads|ja|管玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tube beads|qq=}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|en|Itoigawa|WD=Q857208|lt=Itoigawa}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.<ref>Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}}</ref>
The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|WD=Q4557237|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum}} and Kyoto University Museum.
== designation1 == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture designation1 (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002.
== Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes.
== References == <references/>

== Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes.
== References == <references/>
== External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Intypeation Site]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shikinzan Kofun]][[ja:紫金山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shikinzan Kofun]][[ja:紫金山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shikinzan Kofun]][[ja:紫金山古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shikinzan Kofun | image = File:Shikinzan Kofun, funcho.jpg | location = {{ill|Ibaraki|WD=Q502403}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.844222|N|135.546056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) | area = Approximately110 meters in total length | built = Early 4th century | burial = | Interred_individual = | burial goods = | designation1 = Osaka Prefecture designation1 }}
'''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in [[Ibaraki City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Tottori Prefectural Museum|en|Tottori Prefectural Museum|WD=Q4707652|lt=Tottori Prefectural Museum}}.}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|en|Kyoto University|WD=Q336264|lt=Kyoto University}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities.
The mound is an east-facing zenpokoenfun constructed using the ridge of a hill. It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top.
burial goods excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃|1=Iron arrowheads|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron arrowheads|qq=}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.<ref>Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}}</ref>
== Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters.
Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuoji River|1=Katsuōji River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400222}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki River|1=Ibaraki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617858}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like a11endages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture.
== burial goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡|1=Han-style mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese bronze mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Han-style mirror|qq=}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.
Other notable grave goods included the oldest type of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|Kudatama|lt=Kudatama|lt=Tube beads|ja|管玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tube beads|qq=}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|en|Itoigawa|WD=Q857208|lt=Itoigawa}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.<ref>Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}}</ref>
The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|WD=Q4557237|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum}} and Kyoto University Museum.
== designation1 == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture designation1 (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002.
== Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes.
== References == <references/>
== External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Intypeation Site]

A north-south aligned {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} was discovered at the center of the rear mound. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top.
burial goods excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃|1=Iron arrowheads|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron arrowheads|qq=}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.<ref>Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}}</ref>
== Subsequent Investigations == In 1992 (Heisei 4), the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education conducted a second survey involving mound measurement and radar scanning. Additional trench surveys conducted between 2003 and 2004 (Heisei 15–16) confirmed that the mound's total length is approximately 110 meters.
Stone facing (''fukiishi'') from the mound slopes was determined to be composed of hornfels, sandstone, and granite collected from nearby {{ill|Katsuoji River|ja|勝尾寺川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuoji River|1=Katsuōji River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400222}} and {{ill|Ibaraki River|ja|茨木川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki River|1=Ibaraki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617858}} riverbeds. A significant number of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were unearthed, including some with wing-like a11endages similar to those from the Matsutakeyama Kofun in Osaka Prefecture.
== burial goods == The mirrors excavated included: * Inside the coffin: {{ill|Chinese bronze mirrors|ja|漢鏡|1=Han-style mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese bronze mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Han-style mirror|qq=}} with four guardian deities in a square design. * Outside the coffin: {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} and imitation triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors.
Other notable grave goods included the oldest type of cylindrical bronze vessels, three unique shell bracelets, and beads such as {{ill|Kudatama|lt=Kudatama|lt=Tube beads|ja|管玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tube beads|qq=}}, natsume beads, and {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama|1=Magatama}}. The natsume beads and magatama were likely crafted from jade sourced from {{ill|Itoigawa|en|Itoigawa|WD=Q857208|lt=Itoigawa|1=Itoigawa}}, Niigata Prefecture. A variety of weapons were also discovered, including 73 swords and 165 iron arrowheads, all found within the stone chamber. However, no bronze arrowheads were found. Armor, such as lamellar cuirasses (''tanko'') and gauntlets, were also notable finds.<ref>Kyoto University Graduate School of Letters, Archaeology Research Laboratory, "Study of the Shikinzan Kofun" (2007) {{National Bibliography Number|21296018}}</ref>
The grave goods are currently preserved and exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|WD=Q4557237|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|1=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum}} and Kyoto University Museum.
== designation1 == * Designated as an Osaka Prefecture designation1 (Historic Site) on January 29, 2002.
== Access == * From [[JR Ibaraki Station]], take the [[Hankyu Bus]] to "Fukui" (heading to Ibaraki Sunny Town), then walk 18 minutes.
== References == <references/>
== External Links == * [http://www.ibaraki-catch.jp/kanko/kanko_c/tya_c/tya_s.html Ibaraki City Tourism Intypeation Site]

'''Shikinzan Kofun''' (紫金山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in [[Ibaraki City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Overview == [[File:紫金山古墳出土 勾玉文帯神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Magatama motif divine beast mirror}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Tottori Prefectural Museum|en|Tottori Prefectural Museum|WD=Q4707652|lt=Tottori Prefectural Museum|1=Tottori Prefectural Museum}}.}}]] In 1947 (Shōwa 22), during construction of a hospital facility at the hill’s summit, a portion of the {{ill|stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} was exposed. An excavation was subsequently conducted in May by {{ill|Kyoto University|en|Kyoto University|WD=Q336264|lt=Kyoto University|1=Kyoto University}} and the Osaka Prefecture authorities.

It measures approximately 102 meters in total length, with the rear circular mound having a diameter of about 76 meters and the front rectangular section a width of about 40 meters. Measuring approximately 9 meters in length and 4 meters in width, the chamber was constructed on a U-shaped clay floor surrounded by flatly split andesite and schist stones, with seven granite ceiling stones covering the top.
burial goods excavated from the chamber included beads, swords, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃|1=Iron arrowheads|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron arrowheads|qq=}}, iron sickles, axes, adzes, chisels, and a variety of unique objects, including wheel-shaped stones, sickle-shaped stones, cylindrical bronze vessels, and two shell sickle-shaped stones.<ref>Ibaraki City Board of Education, "Waga-machi Ibaraki: Kofun Edition" (1990) {{National Bibliography Number|90047602}}</ref>
